rulings.Case Title: HOEM & ASSOCIATES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant And Cross-Complainant Maxxon Corporation'S Joinder To Cross-Complainant And Cross-Defendant Hoem And Associates' Motion To Reopen Limited Discovery Due To Necessity Of Trial Continuance
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 14, 2019, Line 1, Cross-Defendant And Cross-Complainant Maxxon Corporation'S Joinder To Cross-Complainant And Cross-Defendant Hoem And Associates' Motion To Reopen Limited Discovery Due To Necessity Of Trial Continuance. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. (specially set at 11:00 am, before Judge Pro Tem Stein) Parties are requested to wait in the hallway if the Department is in use at the 11:00 a.m. start time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LIAN TONG, LLC VS. BING WU CONSTRUCTION COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Bing Wu And Kai Ming Leis Second Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 14, 2019, Line 3, CROSS-DEFENDANT LIAN TONG, LLC'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Bing Wu And Kai Ming Leis Second Amended Cross Complaint. Lian Tong LLC's motion to strike is denied. The court exercises its discretion and will hear the motion even though it was filed late. The court denies Lian Tong's request to strike the claims for $400,000, $61,600, and $20,500. The claimed damages relate to the misrepresentation claims and a factual issue remains regarding what can be recovered. (See Civ. Code Sec. 3333 ["For the breach of an obligation not arising from contract, the measure of damages, except where otherwise expressly provided by this Code, is the amount which will compensate for all the detriment proximately caused thereby, whether it could have been anticipated or not."].) The court denies the request to strike paragraphs 110 and 112 of the Second Amended Cross-Complaint. As explained in the companion motion, the claim for rescission is well-pleaded and the court's prior order contained a typographical error. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Bing Wu is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LIAN TONG, LLC VS. BING WU CONSTRUCTION COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 14, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF LIAN TONG, LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Cross-defendant Lian Tong, LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. The motion is premature because no answer is on file, but the court will (again) exercise its discretion to hear it. (See Code Civ. Proc. Secs. 438(f)(2), 438(a)(2).) Lian Tong fails to show that Bing Wu's claims are time-barred. Lian Tong's original complaint was filed on 2/26/16 and alleges that Bing Wu breached the 2014 construction contract. Because the claims in the Second Amended Cross Complaint also arise out of the 2014 construction contract, they are compulsory and relate back to the filing of the original complaint. (See Sidney v. Superior Court (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 710, 714; Boyer v. Jensen (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 62, 69-70 ["[T]he filing of his complaint caused the statute of limitations to be tolled or suspended as to causes of action arising out of the same set of facts alleged in the complaint. [Citation.] The principle underlying the rule is that the plaintiff has [by filing the complaint] thereby waived the [statute of limitations] claim and permitted the defendant to make all proper defenses to the cause of action pleaded." (internal quotations omitted)].) Therefore, Lian Tong's discussion regarding when the original or amended cross-complaints were filed is irrelevant. The court rejects Lian Tong's argument that Bing Wu has "admitted" that the 2015 cancellation agreement did not "rescind" the 2014 construction contract. The court further rejects Lian Tong's litigation privilege argument. The court previously rejected these assertions. (See 5/14/19 Order, 3:1-4; 3:21-4:3.) Moreover, those arguments improperly seek to dispose of only part of a cause of action. The court denies judgment on the pleadings on the rescission cause of action. Even if Bing Wu cannot recover attorney's fees as damages on the 2015 cancellation agreement, it can sue for rescission of the 2014 construction agreement based on the terms of the 2015 agreement. The court's 5/14/19 order meant to allow Bing Wu to plead a claim for rescission of the 2014 agreement. Its language referring to rescinding the 2015 cancellation agreement was a typographical error. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Bing Wu is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MELYNDA SINGH VS. TOTAL RENAL CARE INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 14, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS MELYNDA SINGH, NARCISSO SANTOR, ANNA SIBAL, JAY SINSON, AND ROBERT TAJANLANGIT'S Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement. Plaintiffs Melynda Singh, et al.'s motion for preliminary approval of class settlement is denied without prejudice to a further evidentiary showing, as follows: 1. Plaintiffs shall provide evidence regarding the experience of KCC Class Action Services sufficient for the Court to conclude that it is qualified to serve as settlement administrator. 2. Plaintiffs shall provide additional information to support the proposed service awards to the Plaintiff class representatives ($15,000 to Plaintiff Melynda Singh, $10,000 to the other four named plaintiffs). The moving papers do not provide any detailed individualized showing as to whether each class representative was deposed, whether he or she provided assistance to class counsel in connection with any aspect of the litigation (and, if so, for how many hours), attended the two mediation(s), etc. (Cf. In re Cellphone Termination Fee Cases (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1380, 1393-1395 [trial court did not abuse its discretion in approving $10,000 incentive awards to four class representatives based on evidence that they had, over the course of the litigation, assisted with investigation, responded to discovery requests, reviewed documents and pleadings, and testified either in deposition or at trial].) 3. Unless plaintiff Melynda Singh's participation in the litigation differed materially from that of the other class representatives, her proposed service payment shall be reduced, and the balance shall be allocated to the release of her individual claims. 4. In light of the size of the class (17,000), plaintiffs should address (a) how many class members are current employees of defendants; and (b) whether the proposed class notice procedure should include a website or other online component other than posting the notice itself on counsel's website. 5. Although no further showing is required at this time, in its motion for an award of attorneys' fees and costs to be heard at the time of final approval, class counsel shall submit declarations of counsel and/or other evidence sufficient to show counsel's lodestar so that the Court may conduct a cross-check of the reasonableness of the requested percentage-based fee. (See Laffitte v. Robert Half International, Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 480, 503-506.) Counsel are requested to appear to set a further briefing and hearing schedule. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE RAMOS VS. WINSTON & STRAWN, LLP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceeding Pending United States Supreme Court Review Of Defendants Petition For Writ Of Certiorari
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 14, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT WINSTON & STRAWN, LLP'S Motion To Stay Proceeding Pending United States Supreme Court Review Of Defendants Petition For Writ Of Certiorari. Defendant Winston & Strawn LLP's motion for stay is granted. The court exercises its discretion per Code of Civ. Proc. Secs. 128(a)(8) and 187 and stays this case through October 7, 2019. The court believes that it is appropriate to grant defendant's short stay request to see if the U.S. Supreme Court grants its cert. petition. The issue regarding the scope of FAA preemption and its effect on Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83 has been the subject of much recent discussion and the Supreme Court may very well grant defendant's petition. A stay is appropriate where "there is some room for candid disputation" and the prejudice to the plaintiff is minimal. (Reynolds v. E. Clemens Horst Co. (1918) 36 Cal.App. 529, 530.) The court determines that granting the short stay will minimally prejudice plaintiff. If for some reason the U.S. Supreme Court does not act on defendant's petition by October 7, 2019, the stay will be lifted and the litigation will proceed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DARRELL ARCHER VS. COINBASE INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 14, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT COINBASE INC.'S Motion For Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs. The motion of defendant Coinbase, Inc. for an award of attorney's fees and costs is denied. The User Agreement between the parties provides, "The prevailing party in any action or proceeding to enforce this Agreement shall be entitled to costs and attorneys' fees." Although plaintiff Darrell Archer's claims included a claim for breach of contract, he did not allege that Coinbase breached the User Agreement, but instead alleged that a contract was established when he opened his account with Coinbase "based on the representations of defendant as holding itself out as a cryptocurrency trading and storage exchange and platform for transactions." (FAC Para. 53.) In granting summary judgment for Coinbase, the Court found the terms of the User Agreement to be dispositive. However, because plaintiff's claims did not constitute an action to enforce that Agreement, this is not "an action on a contract" within the meaning of Civil Code section 1717. (Mountain Air Enterprises, LLC v. Sundowner Towers, LLC (2017) 3 Cal.5th 744, 752-756 [defendant's assertion of an affirmative defense based on written contract did not constitute "action" or "proceeding" within meaning of contract's attorney's fees provision]; see also Gil v. Mansano (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 739, 744 ["Where a contract authorizes an award of attorney fees in an action to enforce any provision of the contract, a defense to a tort action based on a provision of the contract may have the effect of enforcing the provisions of the contract. However, the assertion of a defense does not constitute the bringing of an action to accomplish that goal. Raising a defense may not be equated with bringing an action. The language 'brings an action to enforce the contract' is quite narrow." [citations omitted]]; Exxess Electronix v. Heger Realty Corp. (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 698, 712 & fn. 15 [same]; see generally California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2018) Para. 17:896 ["When the [contractual fee] provision covers only actions 'on the contract' or 'to enforce the contract,' there can be no recovery for services on other claims."].) Although Coinbase is not entitled to recover its costs under Civil Code section 1717, it is a prevailing party and is entitled to recover its costs as a matter of right by filing a memorandum of costs. (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1032; Cal. R. Ct. 3.1700.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Mr. Archer is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JHPDE FINANCE 1, LLC VS. HA T JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 14, 2019, Line 11, PLAINTIFF JHPDE FINANCE 1, LLC'S Motion For Judgment on the Pleadings. Grant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The Court will sign the proposed order and judgment included with the moving papers. =(302/EPS)
